assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Checks are limited to reset-safe, protocol-safe, or output-visible behavior.
No fixed latency or exact cycle count is assumed.
dout updates combinationally with addr changes
cases.goal: Validate combinational read behavior across arbitrary addresses
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Since ROM is combinational, dout should reflect mem[addr] without a clock.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Only deterministic, contract-supported stimulus is used; otherwise the case is downgraded.
Drive a set of diverse address values and monitor dout
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Observe dout within the same simulation delta as addr change (no clock required)

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
dout matches known initialized values for addresses 0, 1, 2, 3
cases.goal: Check boundary addresses and known data values
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Addresses 0x00 and 0xFF test lower and upper bounds; also test initialized locations 0x00‑0x03.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['addr']
Apply addr = 0x00, 0xFF, and the initialized addresses 0x00‑0x03
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Observe dout within the same simulation delta as addr change (no latency assumptions)

expected_properties: dout follows each addr change without spurious glitches
cases.goal: Verify ROM output stability under fast successive address changes
notes: Sequence of address values applied without idle cycles
stimulus_intent: Apply a rapid sequence of address values (e.g., 0x00, 0x01, ..., 0xFF)
timing_assumptions: Combinational response expected; no latency assumptions

cases.defer_reason: Advanced derived cases were downgraded because the contract does not yet justify deterministic mainline semantics.
expected_properties: Identical address sequences produce identical dout sequences across runs
cases.goal: Confirm deterministic behavior of ROM across simulation runs
notes: Run two simulations with same address stimulus and compare dout
Advanced derived cases were downgraded because the contract does not yet justify deterministic mainline semantics.
stimulus_intent: Drive identical address pattern in two independent runs

unresolved_items: Timing model is unresolved; generated cases avoid fixed-latency checks.
Contract strength is limited; generated cases favor safe observation over precise timing assumptions.
Negative or illegal-input behavior is not planned because the contract does not provide reliable constraints.
